content.full_text:
March 30, 1977

William McCormick  
Republican Central Committee  
of Los Angeles County  
619 South New Hampshire  
Los Angeles, CA 90005

Dear Mr. McCormick:

This letter concerns our telephone conversation of March 25, 1977. You asked several questions concerning a form letter that the Los Angeles County Republican Central Committee routinely sends to Republicans who have been contacted in a telephone solicitation. The letter expresses appreciation for the recipient's financial support of the "Loyalty Club" and states that the funds contributed will be used to help register and reregister Republicans, finance task forces for special elections, support Republican candidates, and provide public information regarding issues of interest to Republicans. Approximately 500 letters are distributed each month. Based on these facts, you asked whether a copy of the mailing must be sent to the Commission every month. It is the staff's conclusion that the letter "supports state candidates" within the meaning of Government Code Section 84305 and that you should send a copy of the letter to the Commission, indicating that the letter will be used repeatedly. You are not required to submit more than a single copy of the letter so long as the letter remains the same.

You also asked whether another copy should have been sent to the Commission when the signatory changes but the content remains the same. In the interests of full disclosure sought to be served by Section 84305, it would be advisable for you to send another copy to the Commission when a different person begins to sign the letters. However, failure to do so would certainly not constitute a material violation of the Act.

Finally, you asked whether a form letter that is sent to Republicans not reached over the telephone should be sent to the Commission. Assuming that the content of the letter

William McCormick  
Page Two  
March 30, 1977

is similar to the communication discussed above, you should send a copy to the Commission but you are not required to submit any subsequent copies so long as the same form letter is used.

I hope this information will be of assistance.

Sincerely,

Natalie E. West  
Staff Counsel

NEW: jo

March 31, 1977

Mr. Gerald F. Weiner  
Weiner & Rotman  
8399 Topanga Canyon Boulevard  
Canoga Park, CA 91304

Dear Mr. Weiner:

This letter is in response to your inquiry of March 21, 1977, concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.

The Act does not require a committee that is unauthorized by the candidate to state such a fact in its literature. The Act does, however, contain specific requirements which are described in the attached fact sheet, regarding the identification of the sender of mass mailings.

Your understanding that a committee may be composed of a single person is correct.

I hope this information is of assistance. If you have further questions, please contact me.

Sincerely,

Nan Emmerson  
Special Compliance Representative

Enc.

NE:jg
